results.title: VerdantCloud - Vendor Intake & Security Profile
results.snippet: Review VerdantCloud's security certifications, data protection measures, infrastructure controls, and any known gaps.

pages.title: VerdantCloud - Vendor Intake & Security Profile
pages.content:
VerdantCloud Security Intake Profile

Company Overview
VerdantCloud provides cloud infrastructure and managed services with a focus on energy-efficient data centers across North America, Europe, and Asia-Pacific. Headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, we serve over 300 enterprise customers in regulated industries, including financial services and healthcare.

Security Certifications & Attestations
- SOC 2 Type II: Audit completed by TrustAssure LLP, covering security, availability, and confidentiality trust service criteria. Latest report period: July 2024 – June 2025, unqualified opinion. Next audit starts July 2025.
- ISO/IEC 27001:2022: Certificate IS 789012, issued by CertiGlobal, valid through December 2026. Scope includes all cloud platform operations and corporate IT.
- CSA STAR Level 1: Self-assessment submitted and listed on the Cloud Security Alliance registry.
- PCI DSS: Not directly applicable; customers processing cardholder data may use our dedicated compliant hosting environment upon request.

Data Protection & Privacy
- Encryption in transit: TLS 1.3 enforced for all customer-facing and internal APIs. Legacy TLS 1.2 disabled as of February 2025.
- Encryption at rest: AES-256 used for all block and object storage in US East, US West, and EU (Frankfurt) regions. Rollout to APAC (Singapore, Sydney) is in progress, with completion expected in Q4 2025. Relational database offerings currently use transparent data encryption (TDE) where supported by the engine; some older MySQL instances on version 5.7 are scheduled for migration to 8.0 with TDE by Q3 2025.
- Data residency: Customer data stays within the region selected unless explicit customer approval is obtained for cross-region replication.
- Privacy program: GDPR-compliant, with EU-US Data Privacy Framework certification. Annual privacy impact assessments conducted. Data retention and deletion policies published in our Trust Center.

Infrastructure & Network Security
- Physical security: Co-location data centers meet Tier III standards, with 24/7 on-site security personnel, biometric access controls, and video surveillance with 90-day retention.
- Network segmentation: Production, staging, and corporate environments separated via VPCs and firewalls. Micro-segmentation enforced for high-sensitivity workloads.
- DDoS protection: Always-on layer 3/4 mitigation via upstream providers; layer 7 protection through a managed WAF service.
- Vulnerability management: Continuous scanning with weekly prioritized patches. Critical vulnerabilities patched within 48 hours.

Penetration Testing
- Annual external and internal network penetration tests conducted by independent firm NetSecure Labs. Most recent test: Q2 2024. The 2025 test has been scheduled for Q3 2025; the delay was due to a transition to a new testing vendor and is documented in our risk register. Cloud API penetration testing is on a separate schedule and is currently overdue; the last test was Q1 2024, and the next is planned for Q3 2025 alongside the network test.

Incident Response & Business Continuity
- Documented incident response plan aligned with NIST SP 800-61. Annual tabletop exercises; most recent was March 2025.
- 24/7 Security Operations Center (SOC) staffed internally, with escalation to external DFIR retainer if needed.
- Business continuity and disaster recovery plans tested semi-annually. Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for critical services is 4 hours; Recovery Point Objective (RPO) is 1 hour.

Third-Party Risk Management
- Vendor security assessments conducted before onboarding and annually thereafter. Our CDN provider's security review is currently in progress and expected to conclude by end of Q3 2025.
- Supply chain risk monitored through a threat intelligence feed and quarterly review meetings.

Identity & Access Management
- Single sign-on (SSO) with SAML 2.0 and OIDC support. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) enforced for all administrative access.
- Role-based access control (RBAC) with quarterly entitlement reviews. Privileged access management (PAM) using a dedicated vault solution.

Compliance & Governance
- Regular internal audits and monthly management reviews. Policy exception process documented in our Information Security Policy (ISP).
- Current known gaps tracked in a centrally managed risk register, including the overdue API penetration test and pending APAC encryption rollout. Both items have approved risk acceptance until the target completion dates.

For further details or to request specific audit artifacts, contact vendor.risk@verdantcloud.example.com.

Last updated: May 2025
